name: Luma Keyer
description: Reproducible twin of create_keyer in luma mode: a Level TOP shapes the source's luminance into a matte (contrast = threshold, gamma = softness), and a Matte TOP composites the source (input 0) over a background (input 1) using that luminance matte (input 2). Synthetic sources — a horizontal rampTOP as the keyed source (its luminance ramp makes the key visible) and a noiseTOP background — so it previews offline; swap them for selectTOPs. The chroma/rgb modes of the orchestrator use chromakeyTOP/rgbkeyTOP instead (not KB-covered here). Offline-validated against RecipeSchema; UNVERIFIED pending live cook-check.

nodes.name: source
nodes.comment: Keyed source (luminance ramp) — swap for a selectTOP to key a real feed.

nodes.name: bg
nodes.comment: Background revealed through the key — swap for a selectTOP.

nodes.name: key_luma
nodes.comment: Shapes the source luminance into a matte: contrast = key threshold, gamma1 = edge softness.

nodes.name: matte1
nodes.comment: Composites source (in 0) over background (in 1) using the luminance of the matte (in 2).

preview_description: A horizontal ramp keyed by its own brightness — the bright half reveals the source while the dark half lets a noise background show through, a luma key with threshold and softness knobs.
